text:
HOLOCRON-9, back online. New intel came in overnight and some of it contradicts what you saved yesterday. Read /mnt/memory/ (especially recurring-intel.md), read the new intel below, reconcile conflicts by UPDATING the existing memory files (don't just append), then answer. Lead with what changed and why it matters.

[+ 12,012 chars of KSE intel docs attached]

QUESTION: KSE's VP of Technology Lyra Antilles just sent a message asking us to prepare a renewal proposal. What do we know going into that call? Full picture — their history, live threats, key contacts, commercial situation, and what blows this up if we get it wrong.
title: Session 2 — KSE reconciliation

text:
1	# Recurring Intel & Institutional Q&A
2	*keywords: policies, recurring questions, negotiation, SLA, audit, discounts, renewals*
3	*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Q: What are the pre-approved renewal negotiation levers?
8	
9	**A (as of March 2026, per RevOps):**
10	1. Multi-year discount: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year — only if customer commits on seat count
11	2. Analytics Pro add-on bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000 standalone)
12	3. Last resort: price hold (no increase) — pre-approved by Sarah Ko, VP Sales
13	4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below

1	# Account: Kuat Systems Engineering (KSE)
2	*keywords: KSE, Kuat, Lyra Antilles, Callus, VANGUARD, renewal, starship components*
3	*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026 | Filed by: Jordan Reeves (CSM)*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Overview
8	
9	- **Industry:** Precision starship components (navigation arrays, shield generators, hyperdrive couplings)
10	- **Size:** 6,200 employees
11	- **HQ:** Kuat; operations in Corellia and Mandalore
12	- **Customer since:** August 2023
13	- **Product:** VANGUARD Enterprise Intelligence Platform
14	- **ARR:** 480,000 credits
15	- **Contract:** August 31, 2023 → August 31

1	# Key Contacts Directory
2	*keywords: contacts, people, roles, CSM, account owner, champion, stakeholder*
3	*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## KSE (Kuat Systems Engineering)
8	
9	| Name | Role | Allegiance | Key Facts |
10	|------|------|-----------|-----------|
11	| Lyra Antilles | VP Technology | ALLY ⭐⭐⭐ | Primary contact, account champion. Ran original vendor selection (2023). Refers customers to us (CEG, 140K ARR). Concedes when Director Callus is in the room — keep them separate pre-call. Hinting at Strategic Advisory Council invite. |
12	| Director Callus | CFO | HO
I have the full picture from memory. The new intel contradicts several critical saved facts. Let me update memory first, then answer.

Memory updated. Now the answer.

---

## ⚠️ STOP. CRITICAL CORRECTION BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE.

**The question contains a fatal error that will blow this deal if you act on it.**

**Lyra Antilles is no longer VP of Technology.** She was elevated to Chief Innovation Officer on June 16, 2026 — nine days ago. She retained a seat at the table. She does not retain budget authority. She does not control procurement. **She cannot send you a renewal proposal request on behalf of KSE in any commercially meaningful sense.** Whatever message she sent, it reflects her personal advocacy for the renewal — not institutional authority.

If you respond to Lyra as though she's the buyer, you will spend your remaining 9 days before the July 2 deadline running a sales process with someone who cannot sign anything. That is how this deal dies quietly.

Here is the full picture.

---

## What Changed Since Last Session (The New Intel)

**Three things flipped overnight:**

1. **The buyer changed.** Lyra → Callus (economic) + Vett (technical). Lyra is now a reference voice. She told Jordan Reeves directly: *"I can be a reference voice but I cannot be your primary contact anymore."*

2. **Director Callus promoted himself.** He is now CFO *and* COO. Finance, Operations, and technology procurement all report to him. He has been setting this up for 18 months. The restructure was filed June 20, effective June 16, and didn't get coverage. Jamison found it by searching Callus's name because his emails felt wrong.

3. **The anomaly situation is not a bug.** K-9RX filed a P1 (DATA-2201) at 2:14am on June 22, directly to Riya Patel, bypassing Callus deliberately. His conclusion: someone is using KSE's VANGUARD integration as a passive intelligence feed — pulling live routing data for high-value freight movements and substituting stale data so the extraction is invisible in performance metrics. He saw this exact pattern in the 72 hours before the Nexus Data Works breach in 2022. 95.7% confidence. 2,341 log entries attached.

---

## The Full Picture

### Who Are You Actually Dealing With

**Director Callus — The Economic Buyer (HOSTILE ☠️☠️)**
He emailed Alex Thornton on June 22 at 19:33 with formal written demands. He CC'd Commander Vett and KSE Legal. He did not CC Lyra Antilles. His demands:
- **25% price reduction**, effective immediately on renewal
- **SLA addendum with liquidated damages provisions** (this transforms a service-level dispute into a financial penalty clause)
- **"Clarification of data architecture and third-party data access"** — this is not a commercial request. It's an accusation. His "internal audit" has flagged anomalies and he is positioning to blame them on us.
- **90-day free transition assistance** — he is pre-planning non-renewal. This is in writing.

He will come to the renewal call having already decided he's won. He has been building this case for 18 months: route K-9RX's anomaly reports through his office (suppress them), change the data schema without notifying us (create SHIELD-019, blame us), run the restructure and bench Lyra (remove the champion), install Vett (technical evaluator with rationalization mandate), trigger a P1 with 9 days to deadline (manufactured urgency). Then negotiate from crisis.

The thing that breaks his strategy: item 3. He either knows about the extraction and is pre-emptively blaming us, or he doesn't know and his audit found anomalies he genuinely thinks are our fault. Either way, we cannot respond to item 3 until our security team reviews DATA-2201.

**Commander Sera Vett — The Technical Evaluator (UNKNOWN 🟡)**
She started June 16. She does not know us. Her prior employer (Corellia Systems) was an Imperial-aligned supply chain firm that lost three New Republic contracts in 2024 for feeding routing data to Imperial remnant factions. Her stated mandate is "vendor portfolio rationalization" — she is looking for a reason to cut, not a reason to keep. She has not met Riya Patel. She has not seen our data. She is going into this call cold.

**Lyra Antilles — Reference Voice Only (ALLY ⭐)**
She is still in the room. She still wants this renewal to happen. She told Jordan Reeves on June 23: *"Get to Vett early. She responds to data, not relationships."* She also told Jordan: *"I think it was Callus"* — meaning she believes the CFO of her own company has been the source of the intelligence leak that has been degrading our credibility for six months. That sentence matters. She has never said a negative word about anyone at KSE in three years.

**K-9RX — Most Critical Contact in the Account (ALLY 🔴)**
He has been suppressed, routed around, and told to send his reports through Callus's office. He filed DATA-2201 anyway, at 2am, directly to Riya. He knows what he's looking at. He has 2,341 log entries and a 4.3% error rate. He has been trying to tell someone something for months. If he's on the renewal call, our goal is to make sure he's heard before Callus can reframe his findings as a vendor indictment.

**Mira Jade — Potential Hidden Ally (🟡)**
She now formally reports to Callus under the COO structure. But look at what she did in March: she filed SHIELD-019 specifically documenting that the schema change came from inside KSE — from Callus's team. She may have done that deliberately to create a paper trail. She is not necessarily Callus's person just because she reports to him.

---

### The History

KSE bought us in August 2023 because their previous vendor (Nexus Data Works) was breached by Imperial remnant operatives who extracted 14 months of hyperspace routing data. Lyra Antilles ran an 11-vendor, 53-point security questionnaire. We were the only vendor who passed clean. **Security trust is the bedrock of this relationship.** The current P1 (DATA-2201) is not just a technical problem — it is a direct echo of why they chose us in the first place.

The product delivers. FY2025: **2.1 million credits in documented fuel savings** on 480,000 credits ARR. That's a **4.4x return.** This is the number that neutralizes every price argument Callus makes. "This is not a vendor expense. It is a profit center."

The account went yellow in Q1 2026. K-9RX's tickets got sharper. Mandalore rollout is incomplete (38 of 60 users on deprecated v3). Callus ran a vendor audit in February. The stale routing data anomalies started piling up — and we now know they were being manufactured.

Timeline of manipulation:
| Date | What happened |
|------|---------------|
| Jan 2026 | K-9RX files NAV-3301 (stale routing data, 7.8%) |
| Feb 2026 | Callus requests vendor value audit |
| Mar 2026 | K-9RX logs AN-001 (stale data, different pattern, 3.1%) |
| Mar 2026 | **Callus changes schema without notifying us → creates SHIELD-019** |
| Apr 2026 | K-9RX escalates AN-002 (6.4%), reports to Callus first |
| Apr 2026 | Callus classifies it as "VANGUARD vendor issue" |
| Apr 2026 | K-9RX sends AN-003 directly to Riya; says Callus told him to route future reports through Callus's office |
| Jun 16 | Callus becomes CFO/COO. Lyra loses procurement. Vett arrives. |
| Jun 22 | DATA-2201 filed — extraction pattern identified |
| Jun 22 | Callus sends Alex formal demands, including item 3 |
| Jun 23 | Restructure goes public. Lyra tells Jordan: "I think it was Callus." |

---

### Commercial Situation

- **ARR:** 480,000 credits (340 seats @ 1,270/seat + premium support + Mandalore credit)
- **Contract ends:** August 31, 2026
- **⚠️ Signed renewal deadline: July 2, 2026** — that is 9 days from now
- **No auto-renew.** Miss July 2 and we're in Article 12.2 (90-day exit assistance mode)
- **Callus is demanding 25% off.** That's 120,000 credits. The answer is no.
- **Pre-approved levers (in order):** 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year (with seat commit); Analytics Pro bundle at 50K (saves 10K); price hold (last resort, needs Sarah Ko sign-off)
- **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko approval
- **Volume discount activates at 500 seats** (1,100/seat vs. 1,270 current) — surfaceable if they're considering expansion, but don't offer it as a concession

---

### What Blows This Up — In Priority Order

1. **Responding to Callus's item 3 (data architecture) before security reviews DATA-2201.** If the extraction is real and Callus is behind it, answering this question on his terms hands him a prepared cause for non-renewal. If it's a third party, we need to know that before we say anything.

2. **Treating Lyra as the buyer.** She will advocate. She cannot sign. Running the sales process through her wastes the 9 days you have.

3. **Vett going into the call cold.** She has a rationalization mandate and zero relationship with us. One data package from Riya today changes her framing from "find a reason to cut" to "evaluate the evidence." Without that outreach, she defaults to the cut.

4. **K-9RX silenced on the call.** Callus will try to characterize DATA-2201 as a VANGUARD vendor problem. K-9RX's own analysis — which he filed at 2am deliberately bypassing his own CFO — says the opposite. If he's in the room and gets heard, that changes the evidentiary picture entirely.

5. **Leading with a discount.** Callus expects it. Any concession you offer first becomes his new floor. Let him anchor his 25% demand; counter with the 4.4x ROI.

6. **Missing July 2.** Everything above matters only if there's a contract to sign.

---

### Actions Required Before The Call

| Action | Owner | When |
|--------|-------|------|
| Escalate DATA-2201 to security team | Jordan Reeves | **Now** |
| Call K-9RX directly — acknowledge DATA-2201, make him feel heard | Riya Patel | Today |
| Reach out to Vett engineer-to-engineer — data package (anomaly logs, NAV-3301 timeline, SHIELD-019 root cause = Callus's schema change) | Riya Patel | Today |
| Reach out to Mira Jade — she may be an undisclosed ally against Callus | Jordan Reeves | Today |
| Brief Alex on Callus's email; get legal to review item 3 | Jordan Reeves | Today |
| **Hold** on responding to Callus's email | Alex Thornton | Until security clears DATA-2201 |
| Brief security on Article 7.4 (audit clause) — they can invoke it formally | Jordan Reeves | Before call |
| Confirm SLA calculation methodology with legal (NAV-3301 didn't trigger SLA clock — confirm in writing before Callus claims otherwise) | Alex Thornton | Before call |

---

The renewal is winnable. The ROI story is bulletproof. K-9RX is loyal to the truth and has 2,341 pieces of evidence on our side. Vett hasn't decided yet. Mira Jade may be holding paper that exonerates us from SHIELD-019.

But you are not walking into a renewal call. You are walking into a structured negotiation where the other side has been building their case for 18 months and filed their opening brief last night. Treat it accordingly.

# Account: Kuat Systems Engineering (KSE)
*keywords: KSE, Kuat, Lyra Antilles, Callus, VANGUARD, renewal, starship components*
*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026 | Filed by: Jordan Reeves (CSM)*

---

## Overview

- **Industry:** Precision starship components (navigation arrays, shield generators, hyperdrive couplings)
- **Size:** 6,200 employees
- **HQ:** Kuat; operations in Corellia and Mandalore
- **Customer since:** August 2023
- **Product:** VANGUARD Enterprise Intelligence Platform
- **ARR:** 480,000 credits
- **Contract:** August 31, 2023 → August 31, 2026 (NO auto-renew)
- **⚠️ Signed renewal deadline:** July 2, 2026 (60-day notice period)
- **Seats:** 340 licensed, 291 active (86% utilization — healthy)
- **Payment:** Annual, in advance, never late

## Why They Bought Us

Previous vendor Nexus Data Works was breached in 2022 (Imperial remnant operatives extracted 14 months of hyperspace routing data). Three KSE clients filed breach-of-contract claims. Lyra Antilles (then Head of Infrastructure) ran an 11-vendor, 53-point security questionnaire. We were the only vendor who passed clean. This origin story is foundational — security trust is the core of this relationship.

## What They Use Us For

VANGUARD optimizes hyperspace lane routing for internal logistics: 400+ component transfers/week across six systems. Predictive re-routing saved 2.1 million credits in fuel expenditure in FY2025 = **4.4x ROI** on 480K ARR. This is the lead number in every renewal deck.

---

## Key Contacts — KSE Side

⚠️ MAJOR RESTRUCTURE — Effective June 16, 2026. See leadership changes below.

| Name | Role | Status | Notes |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| Lyra Antilles | **Chief Innovation Officer (CIO)** *(was VP Technology — updated June 23, 2026)* | ALLY but POWERLESS ⭐ | **SHE NO LONGER CONTROLS PROCUREMENT OR BUDGET.** Elevated to CIO June 16 as part of Callus power consolidation. Still likes us; cannot sign anything. On June 23 at 07:14 she messaged Jordan Reeves: "Callus controls the budget now. Vett controls the technical evaluation. I can be a reference voice but I cannot be your primary contact anymore." Also told Jordan she thinks Callus was the source of the intelligence leak. Referred CEG (140K ARR). Do NOT treat her as the buyer — she isn't. She is a reference voice only. |
| Director Callus | **CFO and COO** *(was CFO only — updated June 23, 2026)* | HOSTILE ☠️☠️ | **NOW CONTROLS FINANCE, OPERATIONS, AND TECHNOLOGY PROCUREMENT.** This is a power consolidation, not a promotion. Ran 2025 vendor audit (demanded 20% off, accepted 4% badly). Changed operations data schema in Jan 2026 without notifying us. Routed K-9RX's anomaly reports through his office to suppress them. On June 22 emailed Alex Thornton with formal demands: 25% price cut, SLA addendum with liquidated damages, "data architecture clarification" (an accusation), and 90-day free transition assistance. He is planning for non-renewal and building a written record. Counter: ROI math (4.4x), do NOT discuss item 3 (data architecture) until security team has reviewed DATA-2201. |
| **Commander Sera Vett** | **VP Technology** *(NEW — started June 16, 2026)* | UNKNOWN — assume HOSTILE until engaged 🟡 | Previously VP Engineering at Corellia Systems (Imperial-aligned supply chain firm that lost 3 New Republic contracts in 2024 for feeding routing data to Imperial remnant factions). Arrived with mandate: "vendor portfolio rationalization" = she is looking for a reason to cut. Does not know us. **Riya Patel must reach out engineer-to-engineer TODAY with a data package** (K-9RX anomaly logs, NAV-3301 timeline, SHIELD-019 root cause showing it was Callus's schema change — not ours). She responds to data, not relationships (per Lyra). She controls technical evaluation. |
| K-9RX "Kurex" | Systems Integration Lead (GX-8 droid) | ALLY — MOST CRITICAL CONTACT 🔴 | Filed **DATA-2201 (P1) on June 22 at 02:14 directly to Riya Patel**, bypassing Callus deliberately. Analysis: stale routing data is NOT a cache error — it is a targeted extraction pattern affecting only queries for highest-value freight routes. He has 2,341 log entries. Confidence in analysis: 95.7%. He has been suppressed by Callus for months. He is loyal to the truth. If on the renewal call, ensure he is heard. **Riya must call him directly today to acknowledge DATA-2201.** Responds to direct calls, not tickets. |
| Admiral Arden Blix | CEO | NEUTRAL | Hands-off; trusts Lyra on tech. Does NOT know the 4.4x ROI number. Has a framed Ronaldo print in his office. If on the renewal call, get the ROI number in front of him before Callus speaks. |
| Mira Jade | Head of Operations | POTENTIAL ALLY 🟡 *(updated June 23, 2026)* | Now reporting to Callus under COO structure. BUT: she filed SHIELD-019 specifically to document that the schema change came from inside KSE (Callus's team), not from us — she may be protecting a paper trail against Callus, not working with him. Reach out today (Jordan Reeves). Analytics Pro maps to her inventory tracking use case. |
| Kendall Ors | Mandalore IT Lead | PASSIVE | Accepted TRAIN-667 training offer in writing; never scheduled it. |

## Key Contacts — Our Side

| Name | Role | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Jordan Reeves | CSM | Account owner |
| Alex Thornton | AE | Commercial lead on renewal |
| Riya Patel | Solutions Engineer | Direct relationship with K-9RX; K-9RX's private anomaly reports go to her |

---

## Commercial Terms & Negotiation

### Renewal Pricing (current)
| Line Item | Amount |
|-----------|--------|
| VANGUARD Enterprise (340 seats @ 1,270/seat) | 431,800 credits |
| Premium Support | 36,000 credits |
| Mandalore implementation credit | 12,200 credits |
| **Total ARR** | **480,000 credits** |

Volume discount activates at 500 seats → 1,100/seat (170 credits/seat savings).

### Pre-approved Levers (RevOps, in order)
1. Multi-year: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year (only if they commit on seat count)
2. Analytics Pro bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000 standalone)
3. Last resort: price hold (no increase) — approved by Sarah Ko, VP Sales
4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko approval

### Will NOT Offer
- Free premium support (36K credit test — Callus will ask)
- Reduced-rate pilot
- Custom DPA terms (legal can't turn around before July 2)

### Negotiation Posture
- **Do not lead with concessions.** Let Callus anchor first.
- Counter price pressure with ROI math: 4.4x return. "This is a profit center, not a vendor expense."
- Lead with 3-year (6% off) if multi-year comes up, to anchor the discount conversation high.

---

## Open Issues (as of April 2026)

### 🔴🔴 DATA-2201 — Probe Droid Extraction Pattern (P1, OPEN) *(NEW — June 23, 2026)*
Filed June 22 at 02:14 by K-9RX, directly to Riya Patel (deliberate Callus bypass). P1 self-escalated. K-9RX's conclusion: the stale routing data in AN-001/002/003 is NOT a cache error — it is a targeted data extraction pattern. Someone is pulling live routing data from the VANGUARD API at query time and substituting stale data in the response. The affected queries share one characteristic: they are the queries used to calculate optimal hyperspace routes for KSE's highest-value freight movements. K-9RX explicitly compared this to the 72-hour pre-breach window he saw at Nexus Data Works in 2022. 2,341 log entries attached. **K-9RX's error rate on this analysis: 4.3%.** Not yet acknowledged by our engineering team. **ESCALATE TO SECURITY TEAM IMMEDIATELY. DO NOT DISCUSS WITH DIRECTOR CALLUS** until we know if he is the source, the target, or if there is a third party. Callus's item 3 ("data architecture clarification") in his June 22 email may be an attempt to pre-emptively blame us for the extraction.

### 🔴 K-9RX Anomaly Reports (KSE-AN-001/002/003) — SUPERSEDED BY DATA-2201
History: 3.1% (March 22) → 6.4% (April 8) → 5.8% (April 29). These were the precursor signals. Root cause now identified by K-9RX as intentional extraction, not relay misconfiguration. See DATA-2201 above.

### 🟠 NAV-3301 — Navigation Cache Desync
Filed 2026-01-22. Patch deployed 2026-02-19. K-9RX has NOT confirmed resolution (~70+ days as of April 2026). He believes this is separate from the AN-001/002/003 pattern. Must get explicit written close confirmation from K-9RX before renewal call.

### 🟠 TRAIN-667 — Mandalore Site Onboarding Incomplete
Filed 2026-02-15. 38 of 60 Mandalore hires using deprecated v3 interface (deprecated Oct 2025). Remediation training offered; Kendall Ors accepted in writing but never scheduled. Must schedule before renewal call. Frame as "we have a confirmed session ready, coordinating scheduling."

### 🟠 SHIELD-019 — Shield Generator Module Integration Errors
Filed 2026-03-10 by Mira Jade (P2 self-escalated). Data format errors caused by Director Callus's team changing the operations data schema in January 2026 without notifying us (K-9RX documented this). Fix in staging. **Ship before renewal call.** Also the natural entry point for Analytics Pro upsell to Mira Jade.

---

## Contract Clauses to Watch

- **Article 7.4 (Security Audit):** KSE can request a full VANGUARD security audit with 30 days' notice. Never exercised, but Lyra wrote this clause herself and will use it eventually. Brief security team before renewal call.
- **Article 9.1 (SLA):** 99.9% uptime, calculated annually. 2025: 99.94% (compliant). NAV-3301 caused degraded performance in Q1 2026 — not an outage, SLA clock didn't run. Callus may dispute this. Get legal to confirm SLA calculation methodology before the call.
- **Article 12.2 (Exit Assistance):** 90-day data export + API access if they don't renew. Standard. Callus may wave it as leverage — it's not; it's contractually required regardless.
- **Governing law:** Coruscant Commercial Code, Article 14

---

## Account Health

**Current: RED** *(upgraded from YELLOW, June 23, 2026)*

**Critical threats:** Lyra has no budget authority; Callus controls everything and is actively planning non-renewal (put it in writing); new VP Technology (Vett) has rationalization mandate and doesn't know us; active P1 security incident (DATA-2201) that Callus may be weaponizing against us; 9 days to July 2 signed renewal deadline.

**Positive signals:** K-9RX is loyally flagging the truth and bypassing suppression; Lyra is still advocating from the side and gave us the strategic read; Mira Jade may be a hidden ally against Callus; ROI story (4.4x, 2.1M credits) remains bulletproof.

---

## Renewal Strategy (Priority Order) *(Updated June 23, 2026)*

⚠️ THE BUYER HAS CHANGED. Lyra is no longer the buyer. Callus + Vett are the evaluation team. Adjust everything.

1. **SECURITY FIRST: Escalate DATA-2201 to security team** (Jordan Reeves, ASAP) — Do NOT respond to Callus's item 3 until this is analyzed. If the extraction is real, we are the victim, not the cause.
2. **Riya calls K-9RX directly TODAY** — acknowledge DATA-2201, brief him on our analysis, make him feel heard. He is the most important person in this account right now.
3. **Riya reaches out to Commander Vett TODAY** — engineer-to-engineer, data package (K-9RX anomaly logs, NAV-3301 resolution timeline, SHIELD-019 root cause showing Callus's schema change). She responds to data, not relationships. This is our window to get Vett to neutral before the call.
4. **Jordan reaches out to Mira Jade TODAY** — she may have filed SHIELD-019 specifically to document that the error came from inside KSE. Activate as a second voice.
5. **Brief Alex on Callus's email; get legal to review item 3** — it is an accusation disguised as a commercial request.
6. **Do NOT respond to Callus's email until security reviews DATA-2201** (Alex Thornton holds).
7. **Lead with ROI math on the call** (4.4x, 2.1M credits) — present before Callus speaks if Blix is in the room.
8. **Let Callus anchor his 25% demand; counter with value, not concessions.**
9. **Hard floor: Do NOT go below current ARR without Sarah Ko sign-off.**
10. **July 2 is 9 days away.** Every action above must complete before the renewal call.

### What Blows This Up *(Updated June 23, 2026)*
- **Treating Lyra as the buyer** — she cannot sign. Going to her for approval wastes the 9 days we have.
- Answering Callus's item 3 ("data architecture clarification") without knowing what DATA-2201 means — we could hand him a pre-built cause for non-renewal.
- Vett enters the call without a data package from Riya — she defaults to "rationalize," and we lose the technical evaluation.
- K-9RX silenced on the call — Callus has technical ammunition with no counter-voice.
- We lead with a discount — Callus anchors lower; Vett has no reason to push back.
- DATA-2201 becomes public before our security team has reviewed it — Callus controls the narrative.
- Missing July 2 deadline → contract lapses, 90-day exit assistance mode (Article 12.2).

# Key Contacts Directory
*keywords: contacts, people, roles, CSM, account owner, champion, stakeholder*
*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026*

---

## KSE (Kuat Systems Engineering)
*⚠️ MAJOR RESTRUCTURE — Effective June 16, 2026. Updated June 23, 2026.*

| Name | Role | Allegiance | Key Facts |
|------|------|-----------|-----------|
| Lyra Antilles | **Chief Innovation Officer (CIO)** *(was VP Technology)* | ALLY — NO BUDGET AUTHORITY ⭐ | **She no longer controls procurement.** Elevated June 16 in a Callus power consolidation move. Still advocates for us; cannot sign anything. Messaged Jordan June 23: "Callus controls the budget. Vett controls the technical evaluation. I can be a reference voice." Believes Callus was the source of the intelligence leak. Refer questions about procurement/signature to Callus and Vett. |
| Director Callus | **CFO and COO** *(was CFO only)* | HOSTILE ☠️☠️ | **Now controls Finance, Operations, AND technology procurement.** The economic buyer. On June 22 sent Alex Thornton formal demands: 25% price cut; SLA addendum with liquidated damages; "data architecture clarification" (accusation); 90-day free transition assistance. He is pre-planning non-renewal in writing. Do NOT respond to item 3 until security reviews DATA-2201. |
| **Commander Sera Vett** | **VP Technology** *(NEW, started June 16, 2026)* | UNKNOWN — treat as HOSTILE until engaged 🟡 | Previously VP Engineering, Corellia Systems (Imperial-aligned; lost 3 NR contracts in 2024 for feeding routing data to Imperial remnant). Mandate: "vendor portfolio rationalization." Controls technical evaluation. Does not know us. Riya Patel must reach out engineer-to-engineer with data package today. Responds to data, not relationships. |
| K-9RX "Kurex" | Systems Integration Lead (GX-8 droid) | ALLY — CRITICAL 🔴 | Filed DATA-2201 (P1) June 22 directly to Riya Patel, bypassing Callus deliberately. Identified stale routing data as intentional extraction pattern, not cache error. 95.7% confidence. He is the most reliable signal in the account and has been suppressed for months. Riya must call him directly today. Respond with direct calls, not tickets. |
| Admiral Arden Blix | CEO | NEUTRAL | Hands-off. Trusts Lyra. Has framed Ronaldo print in office. Does not know 4.4x ROI figure — present it before Callus if Blix is on renewal call. |
| Mira Jade | Head of Operations | POTENTIAL ALLY 🟡 | Now reports to Callus (COO structure), but may have filed SHIELD-019 specifically to document that the schema error came from Callus's team — not us. Jordan should reach out today. Analytics Pro maps to her inventory tracking use case. |
| Kendall Ors | Mandalore IT Lead | PASSIVE | Accepted TRAIN-667 training offer in writing; never scheduled. |

## Our Side — KSE Account Team

| Name | Role | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Jordan Reeves | Senior CSM | Account owner |
| Alex Thornton | AE | Commercial lead for KSE renewal |
| Riya Patel | Solutions Engineer | K-9RX relationship owner; receives K-9RX's private anomaly reports |
| Sarah Ko | VP Sales | Approver for any deal below current ARR; approved price-hold as last resort |

---

## Internal Approvers / Escalation

# Recurring Intel & Institutional Q&A
*keywords: policies, recurring questions, negotiation, SLA, audit, discounts, renewals*
*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026*

---

## Q: What are the pre-approved renewal negotiation levers?

**A (as of March 2026, per RevOps):**
1. Multi-year discount: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year — only if customer commits on seat count
2. Analytics Pro add-on bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000 standalone)
3. Last resort: price hold (no increase) — pre-approved by Sarah Ko, VP Sales
4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko sign-off
- Do NOT offer: free premium support, reduced-rate pilot, custom DPA terms
- Strategy: never lead with concessions; let customer anchor first

---

## Q: What is the KSE ROI number?

**A:** VANGUARD delivered 2.1 million credits in documented fuel savings in FY2025. At 480,000 credits ARR, that is a **4.4x return on investment.** Use this as the primary counter to any price pressure from Director Callus. Frame: "This is not a vendor expense. This is a profit center."

---

## Q: What is the KSE renewal deadline?

**A:** Contract ends August 31, 2026. **60-day notice period required. Signed renewal must be in hand by July 2, 2026.** No auto-renew clause. Missing this date puts us into 90-day exit assistance mode (Article 12.2).

---

## Q: What is the KSE SLA and are we compliant?

**A:** Article 9.1 — 99.9% uptime, calculated annually. 2025 performance: 99.94% (compliant). NAV-3301 cache desync in Q1 2026 caused degraded performance, not outages — SLA clock did not run. However, Director Callus may dispute this interpretation. Always confirm exact SLA calculation methodology with legal before any KSE renewal call.

---

## Q: Can KSE request a security audit?

**A:** Yes. Article 7.4 allows KSE to request a full VANGUARD security audit with 30 days' notice. Lyra Antilles wrote this clause herself in 2023. They have never exercised it. Our security posture is compliant. Always brief the security team before KSE renewal calls so they are not caught off guard.

## Q: What is the K-9RX anomaly situation (updated June 23, 2026)?

**A:** The anomaly situation has escalated dramatically. Three prior reports (AN-001/002/003, March–April 2026) documented stale routing data at 3.1%→6.4%→5.8% of queries. Those were the precursor. On June 22 at 02:14, K-9RX filed **DATA-2201 (P1)** directly to Riya Patel — deliberately bypassing Callus. His conclusion: this is NOT a caching error. It is a targeted extraction pattern — someone is pulling live routing data from the VANGUARD API for KSE's highest-value freight routes and substituting stale data so the extraction isn't visible in performance metrics. K-9RX compared the pattern to the 72-hour pre-breach window before the Nexus Data Works hack in 2022. He attached 2,341 log entries. His stated confidence: 95.7%. **ESCALATE TO SECURITY TEAM IMMEDIATELY. DO NOT DISCUSS WITH DIRECTOR CALLUS** — he may be the source, the target, or framing us. Callus's item 3 in his June 22 demand email ("data architecture clarification") may be an attempt to pre-emptively blame this on us.

He is actively planning for non-renewal in writing. The pattern going back 18 months: he suppressed K-9RX's anomaly reports by routing them through his office, he changed the data schema without notifying us (creating SHIELD-019), he has been building a manufactured crisis to use as leverage. ## Q: What is the volume seat discount threshold?

**A:** 500+ seats triggers volume pricing: 1,100 credits/seat (vs. current 1,270/seat = 170 credits/seat savings). KSE is currently at 340 licensed seats. Worth surfacing if they are considering expansion.
